--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,104 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: d150
+title: 'single-changelog: migrate views to StoreHandle<ViewStore>; delete ViewsChangelog and hand-rolled undo'
+---
+#single-changelog #refactor #views #tech-debt
+
+## Goal
+
+Views become the third domain on the same unified machinery already used by entities and perspectives: text-patch `ChangelogEntry` written by `StoreHandle<ViewStore>`, undo through the global `undo_stack.yaml`. Drop the parallel `ViewsChangelog` + `ViewChangeEntry` (whole-document JSON snapshots) + hand-rolled `undo_entry`.
+
+Independent of the other `single-changelog` cards. Lands whenever it's ready.
+
+## Why this is the right shape
+
+Today views diverge from the unified design in three ways:
+
+- **Storage shape**: `ViewChangeEntry` carries the full `previous` and `current` ViewDef as JSON on every entry (`swissarmyhammer-views/src/changelog.rs:33-37`). For a 5-field edit that means re-storing two whole views — strictly worse than the diffy text patches that `ChangelogEntry` carries.
+- **Undo machinery**: `ViewsChangelog::undo_entry` (`changelog.rs:95-154`) is a hand-rolled match-on-op-then-replay. Doesn't push onto `undo_stack.yaml`. Cmd-Z on a view edit doesn't go on the global undo stack today.
+- **Events**: `swissarmyhammer-views` has no `broadcast`, `subscribe`, or event channel of any kind (verified by grep). The kanban-app has no path for "view-created"/"view-removed" Tauri events. Today, the UI must already have view state and update it client-side; there's no server-side event that announces a view delete or undo-of-delete.
+
+Migrating to `StoreHandle<ViewStore>` fixes all three: the same diff machinery, the same global undo stack, the same `pending_events` model that perspectives already use to surface delete/undo events.
+
+## What
+
+### Add `impl TrackedStore for ViewStore`
+
+Mirror `swissarmyhammer-perspectives/src/store.rs`:
+
+- New `swissarmyhammer-views/src/store.rs`. `ViewStore { root: PathBuf, ... }` with `serialize` / `deserialize` for `ViewDef` ↔ YAML. `extension() = "yaml"`.
+- `impl swissarmyhammer_store::store::sealed::Sealed for ViewStore {}` (same pattern as `EntityTypeStore` and `PerspectiveStore`).
+- `impl TrackedStore for ViewStore { type Item = ViewDef; type ItemId = ViewId; ... }`.
+
+### Wire `StoreHandle<ViewStore>` into `ViewsContext`
+
+Mirror `PerspectiveContext::set_store_handle` (`swissarmyhammer-perspectives/src/context.rs:235-243`):
+
+- Add `store_handle: Option<Arc<StoreHandle<ViewStore>>>` and `store_context: Option<Arc<StoreContext>>` fields on `ViewsContext`.
+- `pub fn set_store_handle(&mut self, handle: Arc<StoreHandle<ViewStore>>)` and `set_store_context`.
+- `ViewsContext::write_view`, `delete_view`, and any other mutation methods delegate to `StoreHandle` when set, falling back to direct file I/O otherwise (matches the existing perspectives pattern for tests).
+- `ViewsContext::reload_from_disk(view_id)` analogue to `PerspectiveContext::reload_from_disk` — re-reads the file and reconciles in-memory state, called from `UndoCmd`/`RedoCmd` after the store layer rewrites bytes.
+
+### Register `StoreHandle<ViewStore>` in `kanban-app::state`
+
+Mirror `register_perspective_store` (`kanban-app/src/state.rs:154-171`):
+
+- New `register_view_store(ctx: &KanbanContext, store_context: &Arc<StoreContext>, kanban_path: &Path)` that constructs `ViewStore::new(...)`, wraps in `StoreHandle`, registers with `StoreContext`, and calls `ViewsContext::set_store_handle` / `set_store_context`.
+- Call it alongside `register_entity_stores` / `register_perspective_store` during app boot.
+
+### Delete the old machinery
+
+- Delete `swissarmyhammer-views/src/changelog.rs` entirely. That removes `ViewChangeEntry`, `ViewChangeOp`, `ViewsChangelog`, the standalone `undo_entry`, `append_changelog`, `read_changelog`, and the `log_create` / `log_update` / `log_delete` helpers.
+- Delete `views_changelog: ViewsChangelog` from `KanbanContext` and the `views.jsonl` plumbing (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs:120` `ViewsChangelog::new(root.join("views.jsonl"))`). Replace the `KanbanContext::views()` accessor with one that pairs `ViewsContext` with `StoreHandle<ViewStore>` instead of `ViewsChangelog`.
+- Delete every Tauri command and entry point that called `ViewsChangelog::log_*` or `undo_entry` directly. They route through the unified `UndoCmd` / `RedoCmd` from `swissarmyhammer-entity::undo_commands` (which delegates to `StoreContext::undo` — the same path perspectives use).
+- Delete `swissarmyhammer-views/src/error.rs::ViewsError::ChangelogEntryNotFound` and `NothingToUndo` (no callers after deletion).
+
+### Wire view events to the UI
+
+`StoreHandle::pending_events` records `ChangeEvent { name: "item-created" | "item-changed" | "item-removed", payload: { store: "views", id: ... } }` on every mutation. The kanban-app already drains `pending_events` for entities / perspectives somewhere in `commands.rs` (or via the bridge — verify at implementation time). Either:
+
+- (A) Reuse the existing pending-events drain to forward view events as Tauri events (`view-created`, `view-changed`, `view-removed`).
+- (B) If the existing drain is entity-only, extend it to handle view events, or add a lightweight bridge in `kanban-app/src/state.rs` that subscribes to `StoreHandle::events()` and forwards.
+
+Pick (A) if the drain is generic; (B) if it's entity-coded.
+
+### Old `views.jsonl` data
+
+If the file exists on disk in a workspace, it's now orphaned. Two options:
+
+- **Drop it on first boot** — the migration code logs a warning and removes the file. View edits already lost their undo history is acceptable because views are configuration, not history-bearing data.
+- **Leave it as orphaned data** — the cleanup card (`single-changelog` orphan cleanup) covers it.
+
+Pick "leave it" — keeps this card scoped to source code; the cleanup card handles the file.
+
+## Acceptance
+
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-views/src/store.rs` exists with `impl TrackedStore for ViewStore`. Mirrors the perspective store's structure.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-views/src/changelog.rs` is **deleted**. `find swissarmyhammer-views/src -name 'changelog.rs'` returns nothing.
+- [ ] `grep -rn 'ViewsChangelog\|ViewChangeEntry\|ViewChangeOp\|views_changelog' --include='*.rs'` returns nothing outside legacy migration tests.
+- [ ] `ViewsContext::write_view` / `delete_view` push onto the global `UndoStack`. Verify by inspection of `undo_stack.yaml` after a view edit — entries with `id: <ULID>` and `label` describing the view operation appear.
+- [ ] `UndoCmd` works on a view edit: write a view, run `UndoCmd`, the previous view state is restored on disk and `ViewsContext` reflects it.
+- [ ] Frontend receives Tauri events for view create / change / remove (via either reused pending-events drain or new view bridge). Confirm by ad-hoc test that the UI updates without manual refresh.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-views -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -p kanban-app` green.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-store` green (no regression in store-layer behavior).
+
+## Tests — focus on delete/undo-delete event roundtrips
+
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-views/src/context.rs` — `write_view_pushes_onto_undo_stack`: regression equivalent to `swissarmyhammer-perspectives::context::write_pushes_onto_undo_stack` (`context.rs:1101`).
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-views/src/context.rs` — `delete_view_pushes_onto_undo_stack`: regression equivalent to `delete_pushes_onto_undo_stack` (`context.rs:1131`).
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-views/src/context.rs` — `undo_view_create_round_trip`: write a view via `ViewsContext::write_view` (delegates to StoreHandle) → assert file exists, undo entry on stack → `StoreContext::undo()` → assert file gone, `ViewsContext::get_by_id` returns None, `pending_events` contains `view-removed`.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-views/src/context.rs` — `undo_view_delete_round_trip`: write → delete → assert file in `.trash/`, `get_by_id` None, `pending_events` `view-removed` → `StoreContext::undo()` → assert file restored, `get_by_id` returns the view, `pending_events` `view-created`. Mirrors `swissarmyhammer-perspectives::context::reload_from_disk_evicts_cache_and_emits_deleted_on_file_absence` and its counterpart.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/src/state.rs` (or wherever the view-events bridge lands) — `bridge_routes_view_undo_of_delete_to_view_created`: end-to-end. Real ViewsContext, real StoreHandle, fake Tauri emitter. Write view → delete → undo. Assert the emitter receives, in order: `view-created`, `view-removed`, `view-created`.
+- [ ] No regression in perspectives' identical roundtrip (sanity check that the shared infrastructure still works for perspectives).
+
+## Workflow
+
+`/tdd`. Start with the four roundtrip tests in `swissarmyhammer-views::context` against the mirror of `PerspectiveStore` — they fail because `ViewStore` doesn't exist yet. Build `ViewStore` + the wiring until they pass. Add the bridge test. Then delete the old `changelog.rs`. Compiler will indicate unused imports and call sites; fix them. Then run the full nextest.
+
+## Scope
+
+- depends_on: nothing.
+- Blocks: the cleanup card (which removes `.kanban/views.jsonl` and any other orphans) — that card depends on this one for the source-code teardown.

 ## Why this is the right shape

 Today views diverge from the unified design in three ways:

-- **Storage shape**: `ViewChangeEntry` carries the full `previous` and `current` ViewDef as JSON on every entry (`swissarmyhammer-views/src/changelog.rs:33-37`). For a 5-field edit that means re-storing two whole views — strictly worse than the diffy text patches that `ChangelogEntry` carries.
-- **Undo machinery**: `ViewsChangelog::undo_entry` (`changelog.rs:95-154`) is a hand-rolled match-on-op-then-replay. Doesn't push onto `undo_stack.yaml`. Cmd-Z on a view edit doesn't go on the global undo stack today.
-- **Events**: `swissarmyhammer-views` has no `broadcast`, `subscribe`, or event channel of any kind (verified by grep). The kanban-app has no path for "view-created"/"view-removed" Tauri events. Today, the UI must already have view state and update it client-side; there's no server-side event that announces a view delete or undo-of-delete.

+
+Migrating to `StoreHandle<ViewStore>` fixes all three.
+
+## Bridge contract: mirror the perspective pattern, not the entity pattern
+
+There are two pre-existing patterns in the codebase for routing domain events to the UI:
+
+- **Entity** uses a per-board `HashSet<(entity_type, id)>` seen-set in the bridge. The bridge decides `entity-created` vs `entity-field-changed` based on membership. Undo of delete emits `EntityChanged` (because the cache reload sees a "new" entity), the bridge sees the key absent from the seen-set, and emits `entity-created`.
+- **Perspective** uses an `is_create: bool` flag carried on the `PerspectiveChanged` event itself (`swissarmyhammer-perspectives/src/events.rs`). `reload_from_disk` always emits `is_create: false`, so undo of delete emits `entity-field-changed` (with `entity_type: "perspective"`). The frontend perspective listener treats any change event for a perspective as "refetch the list."
+
+**This card adopts the perspective pattern for views.**

-Migrating to `StoreHandle<ViewStore>` fixes all three: the same diff machinery, the same global undo stack, the same `pending_events` model that perspectives already use to surface delete/undo events.

+- `ViewsContext` carries `event_sender: broadcast::Sender<ViewEvent>` and emits on `write_view`/`delete_view`/`reload_from_disk`. `reload_from_disk` always emits `is_create: false` — same convention as perspectives.
+- The kanban-app bridge (`kanban-app/src/watcher.rs`) gets a `process_view_event` mirroring `process_perspective_event` (`watcher.rs:802`), routing into the same `entity-created` / `entity-field-changed` / `entity-removed` Tauri channels with `entity_type: "view"`. Frontend treats any `entity_type === "view"` event as a refetch trigger.
+
+Why this pattern: views are configuration, the list is small (typically <10 per board), refetch-on-any-change is cheap, and the existing perspective bridge code is straightforward to mirror. The entity seen-set pattern is overkill for this volume.

-## Bridge contract: mirror the perspective pattern, not the entity pattern
+- **Storage shape**: `ViewChangeEntry` carries the full `previous` and `current` ViewDef as JSON on every entry (`swissarmyhammer-views/src/changelog.rs:33-37`). For a 5-field edit that means re-storing two whole views — strictly worse than the diffy text patches that `ChangelogEntry` carries.
+- **Undo machinery**: `ViewsChangelog::undo_entry` (`changelog.rs:95-154`) is a hand-rolled match-on-op-then-replay. Doesn't push onto `undo_stack.yaml`. Cmd-Z on a view edit doesn't go on the global undo stack today.
+- **Events**: `swissarmyhammer-views` has no `broadcast`, `subscribe`, or event channel of any kind (verified by grep). The kanban-app has no path for "view-created"/"view-removed" Tauri events. Today, the UI must already have view state and update it client-side; there's no server-side event that announces a view delete or undo-of-delete.

-There are two pre-existing patterns in the codebase for routing domain events to the UI:
-
-- **Entity** uses a per-board `HashSet<(entity_type, id)>` seen-set in the bridge. The bridge decides `entity-created` vs `entity-field-changed` based on membership. Undo of delete emits `EntityChanged` (because the cache reload sees a "new" entity), the bridge sees the key absent from the seen-set, and emits `entity-created`.
-- **Perspective** uses an `is_create: bool` flag carried on the `PerspectiveChanged` event itself (`swissarmyhammer-perspectives/src/events.rs`). `reload_from_disk` always emits `is_create: false`, so undo of delete emits `entity-field-changed` (with `entity_type: "perspective"`). The frontend perspective listener treats any change event for a perspective as "refetch the list."
+Migrating to `StoreHandle<ViewStore>` fixes all three: the same diff machinery, the same global undo stack, the same `pending_events` model that perspectives already use to surface delete/undo events.
